articles.title: Prime Minister Worries About Kanye West at Music Festival
articles.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer is worried because Kanye West, a famous rapper, is going to perform at a big music festival in London this summer. Kanye West has said mean things about Jewish people in the past, which is called antisemitism, and he even said nice things about Nazis, who were very bad people in history. The Prime Minister thinks this is not okay and wants everyone to make sure Jewish people feel safe in Britain. The festival's main sponsor, Pepsi, has decided to stop supporting the event because of this. Some other leaders think Kanye West should not be allowed to come to the UK. Kanye West has apologized for his comments before, but people are still concerned. The festival is called Wireless and happens in north London for three days.

articles.title: A Brave Rescue in Iran
articles.summary: A US fighter jet was shot down in Iran, and two crew members had to jump out. One was rescued quickly, but the other was missing. The US military worked hard to find him in the mountains of Iran, where he was hiding from enemies. President Trump said it was a daring rescue mission. The airman was alone for over a day with a handgun, but US officials watched him all the time and planned the rescue. Helicopters and other planes helped in the operation. Finally, the airman was saved and is now safe. This shows how the military trains for tough missions to bring people home.

articles.title: Astronauts See the Moon's Hidden Side for the First Time
articles.summary: A group of astronauts on a mission called Artemis II have seen the far side of the Moon for the first time. The crew includes four people: Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. They are traveling in a spacecraft named Orion and are on their third day of the trip. They took a photo of a special area on the Moon called the Orientale basin, which no human had ever seen completely before. The spacecraft is very far from Earth, over 180,000 miles away. The astronauts described the view as 'absolutely spectacular' and different from the Moon we usually see. This mission is exciting because it helps us learn more about space and prepares for future trips to the Moon.

articles.title: AI Videos in Hungary's Election
articles.summary: In Hungary, a fake video made with AI was shared by Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's party before an election. The video shows a soldier being executed and tries to scare people about the war in Ukraine. It targets Péter Magyar, who is running against Orbán. The video says Magyar wants Hungary to join the war, but Magyar says that is not true. Fact-checkers say using AI to make fake videos is new in elections. This story is about how technology can be used to trick people during important votes.

articles.title: President Trump's Plan for College Sports
articles.summary: President Donald Trump signed an order to help college sports. He wants to make sure schools follow rules about players and money. If schools do not follow the rules, they might lose federal funding. This funding is money from the government that helps schools pay for things. The order talks about eligibility rules, which decide who can play, and transfers, when players move to different schools. College sports have become a big business with players earning millions. Trump asked Congress to act quickly, but it has been stuck for over a year. The NCAA and other groups are involved in making decisions. This order is Trump's second try to fix college sports.

articles.title: Georgia's New Law About Homeless Camps
articles.summary: In Georgia, lawmakers passed a new bill that lets property owners sue local governments. If the governor signs it, people can ask for money if they think their property lost value because the government didn't enforce rules against homeless encampments, loitering, or panhandling. The bill's sponsor says it holds cities accountable for keeping areas clean and safe. But Democrats and homelessness helpers worry it will lead to more arrests of homeless people and wasteful lawsuits. They think money should go to housing instead. A last-minute change also allows lawsuits about immigration policies. Some say this bill is cruel and won't solve homelessness.
